author: gb9t
content:
User-flagged architectural clarification (2026-05-09): grsec absorption is two-layer.

When 00-security-principles.md is eventually authored (after Phase B review), it MUST encode the layered split:

- **Memory protection + CFI (PaX core, AUTOSLAB, RANDSTRUCT, MEMORY_SANITIZE, …)**: mandatory always-on or configurable-with-secure-defaults. LSM-orthogonal. Absorbed.
- **Policy enforcement (GR-RBAC, GRKERNSEC_HIDESYM, GRKERNSEC_HIDETASK, GRKERNSEC_TPE, GRKERNSEC_CHROOT_* policy switches, …)**: NEVER absorbed as defaults. Where the feature has defense-in-depth value, expose as sysctl knob explicitly documented as 'complementary to but never substitute for LSM policy'. The LSM framework AND every in-tree LSM (SELinux, AppArmor, SMACK, Yama, Lockdown, Landlock, etc.) is preserved exactly per drop-in REQ-1.

This means 00-security-principles.md will NOT specify a Rookery RBAC system, will NOT default-enable HIDESYM, will NOT default-enable TPE, will NOT replace SELinux's chroot enforcement with hardcoded grsec rules.

The doc DOES specify:
- Which PaX-equivalent memory protections are always-on (KERNEXEC, USERCOPY, REFCOUNT)
- Which are configurable with secure defaults (RANDKSTACK, MEMORY_SANITIZE-on-free)
- Sysctl knobs for opt-in defense-in-depth (kernel-symbol-hiding stricter than kptr_restrict=2)
- A position on retpolines / IBRS / IBT / CET (matching upstream defaults)

This dependency is now also captured in the security/00-overview.md Hardening section.
